MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - Thank you.
Mr. President, Mr. Vice President, very valuable bureaucrats, dear members of parliament; First of all, the last day of the budget studies discussed in the Planning and Budget Commission was a very intense and grueling marathon. On this occasion, we would like to thank the Commission President, Commission members, stenographers, the staff who brought us tea, and everyone who contributed. Since today is the last day, we consider it a debt of gratitude. An important budget is being discussed; We are discussing the budget of the head of the executive branch. It seems that in the previous and next budgets, we will not see the head of the executive defending this budget. Because, since 2018, that is, since the day we moved to the Presidential government system, we have not witnessed Mr. President, the head of the executive, who is actually the main owner of the budget, coming to the Grand National Assembly of Turkey and participating in these negotiations in order to own this budget; This is a great deficiency. In our opinion, this is a political deficiency, a legal deficiency, a great administrative deficiency, and a very, very great deficiency in terms of the nation's budget right. In our opinion, the person who wants the authority to use the state's money should deign to come here and explain himself. Because the budget is honor, the budget is a very serious issue, both the Chairman of the party and the President; We are discussing an environment where state affairs and political affairs are intertwined through these budget discussions. All political activities are carried out while using the budget, so come and explain to this nation what kind of policy you will follow with this money and this budget, pen by pen. The minimum democratic requirement that makes visible the responsibility of the highest authority of the executive in the face of Parliamentary control is eliminated by the President's absence in person. Again, budget is not a technical issue; It is a political responsibility and program calculation. This issue is very, very important as it shows how meticulously you care about Bethlehem, how and at what level you protect the sustenance of the nation, it is a democratic minimum obligation.
You are looking for legitimacy; If you are looking for legitimacy, the place where you will find that legitimacy is these budget negotiations, where you discuss the nation's budget; The place where you will look for this legitimacy is not the words of Trump or the United States Secretary of State, but these discussions in the Turkish Grand National Assembly and your actual presence here will give you this legitimacy in the eyes of the nation. Budgets are the basic documents of politics; Budgets are perhaps the most important documents of politics, showing how a political structure views the world, events and national issues.
This issue was discussed again last year, and our Chairman of the Planning and Budget Commission, Mr. Erhan Usta, just brought it to the agenda; The President of Religious Affairs, the Director of National Intelligence and the Director of Communications were not there last year, not this year, and not the year before that. In our opinion, their failure to come to the Parliament, where they are supposed to be accountable, is an indication of how much they lack democratic consciousness and mentality, and a clear expression of how much they care about the will of the nation. Why do we care about this issue?

Look, let me ask you this, I'm asking all of you: For example, if the President had said
To the President of Religious Affairs: Come on, let's discuss a few topics regarding this budget. Could he send the Deputy President of Religious Affairs? Or if the President had said to the Head of the National Intelligence Organization: Come on, let's talk about this issue. Could Mr. President say, "I cannot come, I will send my assistant to you?" or could he do this? Those who cannot do this to the President see it as appropriate for the nation. Those who cannot show this courage to the President show this attitude by easily not coming here for these budget talks where the nation's budget is discussed; Then you get angry when we say "one man regime". When the President calls him to the palace - everyone here will give that answer - he says to the President of Religious Affairs, "Come." When he says that, he runs away, but he doesn't come here to account for his budget. There are valuable bureaucrats, they listen, go and report, but this is both disrespectful to us and ignoring the nation, which means not recognizing the will. I am sure that the same attitude will be shown with the same stubbornness next year, because this is now a defiance, we do not see any other meaning or explanation for this.
You are responsible to this country and this nation, you have an obligation to handle these issues in this way; I am telling these to the Presidents of these three institutions. In fact, the Presidential budget, which we will go into detail in a moment, is significant in that it fully reflects a mentality and the character of the current Presidential government system in Türkiye. Because, when you look at it, we are talking about a figure that is not even 1 per thousand of the general budget, but it means a lot in terms of the institution it represents and the details within it. Because, in our opinion, when we look at it, we see that an inconsistent, inconsistent approach that is not transparent, unmonitored, unaccountable, that cares more about its own comfort and reputation than the public and NGOs, that is disconnected from the public, is manifested in this Presidential budget, that there is insincerity, that does not comply with the principles and rules and targets set by itself, and that there is a chaos and confusion of authority just like the Presidential government system. I will share with you why I am saying these in detail in a moment. But before that, I have to make a point here; Because, if we criticize, it is valuable and important for us to express our satisfaction from time to time on issues that we are satisfied with, as it shows the strength, sincerity and consistency of our criticisms. Mr. Vice President also mentioned in his speech that they answered approximately 90 percent of the 1,426 parliamentary questions, most of which were answered after the fifteen-day period, but 90 percent were answered, last year this figure was 88 percent. Look, I don't appreciate it, I express my satisfaction; because this is a constitutional obligation. The reason for our satisfaction is this: When you compare it with other ministries, we see an understanding here that is successful or taken into consideration and shows that it is taken into account.

Especially when you look at the response rate of the Ministry of Justice and the Ministry of Internal Affairs to the parliamentary questions we submitted, the diligence shown by the Vice President and his team brings with it the obligation to express our satisfaction. Again, this is not an appreciation; Because, constitutionally, this is the responsibility of every ministry, but we have come to such a point that where there are ministries that do not answer 5 percent or 10 percent, such performance satisfaction brings with it expression.
Now, the Presidential budget is 21.2 billion, that is, 0.11 percent of the general total budget of 18.9 trillion, that is, 1 per thousand. As I have just said, this budget has value in terms of its content, the meaning it carries and the message it gives to the society, rather than its size. In fact, when you look at other affiliated institutions, the total figure comes to approximately 627 billion TL. That is, when you add other institutions affiliated with the Presidency, we see that it corresponds to 3.3 percent of the total budget. Now, there is talk of a 25 percent increase. Of course, we do not know how and with what figure the Presidential budget will be closed this year; Because, when we look at the tenth month, at the end of October, you have used 72 percent of the budget you gave for 2025. Of course, there may be lunar and seasonal effects, but there is also this strangeness: Here, 600 million, 700 million is spent,
It is spent, it is spent, suddenly there is a terrible jump in October. When you look at it, you get the feeling: Oh, sir, the end of the year is coming, let's use the budget figure we set as soon as possible, so that no unused numbers are left out at the end of the year. I'm not saying you do it like this, but this is the feeling it gives us when we look at it. There is a way to handle this, to eliminate this: Well, this is the Plan and Budget Commission, the Highway Traffic Law comes here, I don't know if it is your job, the Traffic and Highways Law is discussed here, but this place can discuss the following: You can send the realization and budgeted figures of these budgets to the Plan and Budget every three months. You can easily express why your monthly budgets were realized, why they were not realized, where there was a deviation, and in my opinion, the Plan and Budget Commission has fulfilled its primary function in that sense.
There is a projected 61 percent increase in staff. What is this increase? So, will you hire additional staff or will you raise the staff? If so, I wish all public officials and civil servants were met with such a rate, 61 percent, but it seems that 61 percent means that they are talking about hiring additional personnel. We are curious about the details of this, maybe there was one, we missed it, but we will be pleased if it is answered.
The purchase of goods and services was budgeted at 11.7 billion. Of course, people want to see this in pencil, us. I will not take such an approach: You take the total budget, divide it by 365, and then turn around and say, well, I am not saying that this is the daily expenditure item of the Presidency, but you are making you say this. Bring 11.7 billion, that is, you are buying dragon fruit, let people see what you are doing.

If you do not show this openness, this transparency, then when the opposition does not see this clarity, they will make such inferences and from there they will say, "Here, the daily expenditure of the palace is this much." It may lead to a logic like this. Therefore, showing them pencil by pencil. You may not have any bad intentions, but we see that this transparency and the existence of an auditable structure is beneficial to you, as well as to the country, in terms of expressing yourself.
There is only one decreasing figure in the budget, and that is current transfers. Now, when I look at the details of current transfers, I perceive that current transfers are generally money spent for NGOs, that is, for the public good. Every number, every item has increased, and there has been a 20 percent decrease in aid to NGOs compared to last year. So when you look here, there is no saving on our own comfort or reputation, but we see that there is a 20 percent decrease in the item defined as "current transfers" to the public and NGOs; These are items that need explanation. Of course, friends, reputation does not save money. Look, I brought an image with me, I want to share it with you because I find it very meaningful, so there is no need to look for reputation here. Look, that room where the Japanese Prime Minister welcomed Donald Trump, and above that the room where the Japanese Emperor welcomed Donald Trump, there are only two chairs and a table. Above is the meeting of the President - not a foreign mission or anything - with the officials in our own country, this is just a shot from the complex. Look, Japan's national income is 4 trillion dollars, our national income is 1.3 trillion dollars. The national income per capita in Japan is 32,500 dollars, in our country it is 13,400 dollars. Japan's exports are approximately 700 billion dollars, our exports are 262 billion dollars, that is, if we are looking for reputation, not in palaces, splendor and pomp, the most valuable credit we can give to our country, our nation, our state and our citizens is that both national income, national income per capita and our exports will increase when compared to other countries.
Now, when I look at the budget items according to program classification, I get really confused, as I say, there is chaos. There is an item to develop industry, support production and investment, a figure of 965 million has been set for the 2026 budget. Well, we also have a Ministry of Industry and Technology, right? So
Last year, we listened to the Minister of Industry in the Parliament. When we listened, I said: The government forgot the Minister of Industry at home because 90 percent of his speech was about technology. In other words, the country's industry is finished, the country's industry is burning; We haven't heard anything about what kind of precautions will be taken or what will be done.
Now, when we look at the budget of the Ministry of Industry, there is a figure of 89 billion; This is to develop industry and support production and investment. In other words, while an item of 89 billion has been allocated within the Ministry of Industry and Trade, what does it mean that the Presidency allocates a figure of 900 million under the same title, within the same program? Is there any efficiency here, is there not a confusion of authority? Why do both the Ministry and the Presidency allocate budgets under the same title?

You feel like a child who secretly receives pocket money from both his mother and father. The details of this are important.
The same situation exists in the Urbanization and Risk-Oriented Integrated Disaster Management Program. There is an item under the same title in the Ministry of Environment and Urbanization, which has a budget of 30 billion for 2026, and the Presidency said, "Well, I put it there too, we are allocating an allowance of 800 million under the same title." saying.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - They are not the same, Dear Deputy, just different representations.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - They may not be the same, but the titles are the same. So, here, in the Presidential government system, where there are ministries, presidencies, offices, etc., the same budget numbers on the same issue create a question mark in the minds of everyone like us. Of course, they are separate topics, the titles are the same; We would be pleased if you could explain.
There is also a foreign policy item. Of course, on what basis and how is it done? Look, a budget of 4.3 billion is envisaged in 2024. Do you know how much you spent on this pen? 18 million spent; Then, at the end of the year, this 4 billion was added to the Presidency, the Presidency spent 4 billion more, the 4 billion you put for Foreign Affairs was not used, you remained within the budget in total, but there is a serious deviation in the items within the budget. When you look at it, you wonder if there will be such a swing, how can you spend 18 million instead of 4.3 billion? We see the same view this year. A budget of approximately 6.2 billion TL was set, and as of the end of October, there was an expenditure of 900 million. Of course, these are made with a prediction, but it is an indication of how the predictions did not come true and how much they deviated.
When I look at these while preparing the budget, I feel like this, obviously someone did it like this: "So let's put them like this, let's throw them here, so that when the end of the year comes, there will be a need there, there will be a need here, we will take them from there and move them this way." This rule regarding budget shifts between institutions - which has been discussed before in this Commission - needs to be discussed very clearly. Because these transfers bring with them different question marks.
In other words, the system, the Presidential government system, has so many flaws that you see it in the budget items. There are also complications created by this distortion and chaos. Look, there is a Minister of Justice, and there is a Chief Advisor to the President who acts like the Minister of Justice. The Chief Advisor to the President determines the legal agenda in Türkiye on matters related to justice, we learn from there. Hopefully, the Minister of Justice does not learn from him what will happen regarding the law. This consultant has become a name that everyone reads his comments and awaits his predictions when "law" is mentioned in Türkiye, as if he creates jurisprudence on every subject and every issue. So, what do we do with the Minister of Justice then? In other words, I am sharing these examples with you to explain the confusion and chaos in the system.
There are other claims. Here, Mr. Veli Ağbaba touched upon the issue regarding Istanbul Metropolitan Municipality.

In places where all these are happening, of course, there are also allegations that the Istanbul Chief Public Prosecutor is in direct contact with the President and that the Minister of Justice is completely outside of this process, because there is no clarity. Everyone, the consultant says something, the Minister says something; One begins to replace the other, so we see a tangle of institutional problems.
Now, another absurdity in my opinion brought about by this system: Massoud Barzani, who currently does not have an active status in the regional administration in Iraq - he has no status officially.
He came to Türkiye and was welcomed with state protocol. You see, his guards are dressed in military uniforms, with long-barreled weapons in their hands, and they are making a show of power here; Just yesterday, these people were knocking on the door of this state to get a red passport. So, what happened next? There is another advisor to the President, this time he came on stage and said. Of course, we cannot count who is an advisor and who is a chief advisor within this structure, this system. See what Mr. Mustafa Akis says about Mesut Barzani's visit: "As an invited, hospitable nation, Mesut Barzani is invited, and our duty is to welcome him, but there are some protocols that the Republic of Turkey imposes on its guests."
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Here you go.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - ". Currently, Mesut Barzani does not have any duty in the Northern Iraq administration or the Iraqi Central Government. In other words, the protocol that is applied abroad to Abdullah Gül, Ahmet Necdet Sezer or Binali Yıldırım is more or less the same protocol that should be applied to Mr. Barzani." says, the President's advisor says this. "Also, even if a head of state comes to our country, let alone a former government official, his bodyguards, other than the ceremonial officer and his aide, can never wear military uniforms and cannot perform their duties with a long-barreled weapon other than a pistol. Moreover, the Republic of Turkey has the power and power to protect its guests in Şırnak." Here is the picture we all watched together, which was revealed just yesterday by the much-vaunted Presidential government system. And finally, he tagged Şırnak Governorship and said, "These images you made are not appropriate." he writes. In our opinion, this is the problem with the system. If the governors act as party provincial heads and gain honor for themselves, if the chief advisors express their opinions and seek a political starting point, "Is there a palace intrigue underneath the single-party image?" You say, you have to say; That's what's happening today.
You made a presentation last year, I read your presentation. In that presentation, you opened a very important topic about cyber security, and you are right, because the disgraces we have experienced tell us that we need to take serious measures regarding cyber security. Here are the allegations that the data in e-government has leaked out and been taken into someone's hands. While we are talking about these, fake diplomas again.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - I am finishing, President.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Mr. Kavuncu, it is over.

MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - I am finishing.
Lastly, I salute and end.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Come on, greet me.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - Okay.
It is an important topic, and you opened the Cyber ​​Security Presidency in October 2025, that is, as of last month, approximately nine or ten months have passed since its establishment. Hopefully, something like this did not happen: "Well, we said this last year, now they will ask us; let's open it quickly and cut the ribbon in October." In this regard, what security measures will you take regarding this data, what will be done? Because, constitutionally, this is the responsibility of the state; It is your responsibility to protect our data, save it, and prevent it from falling into the hands of others.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Mr Kısacık.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - I am finishing.
Thank you very much. I hope the budget will be beneficial.

records.originalTurkish:
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (İstanbul) - Teşekkür ediyorum.
Sayın Başkan, Sayın Cumhurbaşkanı Yardımcısı, çok değerli, kıymetli bürokratlar, değerli milletvekilleri; öncelikle Plan ve Bütçe Komisyonunda görüşülen bütçe çalışmalarının son günü, çok yoğun, meşakkatli bir maratondu. Bu vesileyle Komisyon Başkanına, Komisyon üyelerine, stenograflara, bizlere çay getiren görevlilere, emeği geçen herkese çok teşekkür ediyoruz. Bugün son gün olması vesilesiyle de bu teşekkürü bir borç olarak biliyoruz. Önemli bir bütçe görüşülüyor; yürütmenin başının bütçesini görüşüyoruz. Buradan bundan önceki ve bundan sonraki bütçelerde de öyle gözüküyor ki biz yürütmenin başını bu bütçeyi savunurken görmeyeceğiz. Zira, 2018'den beri yani Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sistemine geçtiğimiz günden beri biz Sayın Cumhurbaşkanının, yürütmenin başı olan, aslında bütçenin asli sahibi olan Cumhurbaşkanının bu bütçeyi sahiplenmek adına Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisine gelip bu görüşmelere katıldığına şahit olmadık; bu, büyük bir eksikliktir, bize göre bu, siyasi bir eksikliktir, hukuki bir eksikliktir, idari anlamda büyük bir eksikliktir, milletin bütçe hakkı anlamında da çok ama çok büyük bir eksikliktir. Devletin parasını kullanma yetkisini isteyen kişi tenezzül edip de buraya gelip kendini açıklama ihtiyacı bize göre duymalıdır. Zira bütçe namustur, bütçe çok ciddi bir konudur, hem partinin Genel Başkanı hem de Cumhurbaşkanı; devlet işleri ve siyaset işlerinin birbirine girdiği bir ortamı da biz bu bütçe tartışmaları vesilesiyle görüşüyoruz. Bütçeyi kullanırken bütün siyasi faaliyetler yapılıyor, o zaman gelin de bu parayla, bu bütçeyle kalem kalem nasıl bir siyaset izleyeceğinizi bu millete açıklayın. Yürütmenin en üst makamının Meclis denetimi karşısında sorumluluğunu görünür kılan asgari demokratik gereklilik ortadan da kalkmış oluyor Cumhurbaşkanının bizzat gelmemesiyle. Tekrar söylüyorum, bütçe teknik bir mesele değildir; siyasal bir sorumluluk ve program hesabıdır. Beytülmale nasıl titizlenildiğini, milletin rızkına nasıl ve hangi seviyede sahip çıktığınızı göstermesi açısından da bu konu çok ama çok önemlidir, demokratik asgari bir yükümlülüktür.
Meşruiyet arıyorsunuz; meşruiyet arıyorsanız işte o meşruiyeti bulacağınız yer bu bütçe görüşmeleri, milletin bütçesini konuştuğunuz yerdir; bu meşruiyeti arayacağınız yer Trump'ın ya da Amerika Birleşik Devletleri Dışişleri Bakanının sözü değil, Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisindeki bu görüşmeler ve bilfiil burada bulunmanızdır bu meşruiyeti millet nezdinde size verecek olan. Bütçeler siyasetin temel dokümanlarıdır; bir siyasi yapının, dünyaya, olaylara, memleket meselelerine nasıl baktığını gösteren, belki de siyasetin en önemli dokümanlarıdır bütçeler.
Geçen sene bu konu gene konuşuldu, Plan ve Bütçe Komisyonu Başkanımız Sayın Erhan Usta da az önce gündeme getirdi; Diyanet İşleri Başkanı, Millî İstihbarat Başkanı ve İletişim Başkanı geçen sene de yoktu, bu sene de yok, ondan önceki sene de yoklardı. Hesap vermeleri gereken Meclise gelmemeleri demokratik şuur ve zihniyetten de ne kadar yoksun olunduğunun bize göre bir göstergesi, millet iradesini ne kadar önemsediklerinin de net bir ifadesidir. Bu konuyu niye önemsiyoruz? Bakın, şöyle bir sorayım size, hepinize soruyorum: Mesela, Cumhurbaşkanı deseydi ki
Diyanet İşleri Başkanına: Ya, bir gel de şu bütçeyle ilgili birkaç başlığı görüşelim. Diyanet İşleri Başkanı Yardımcısını yollayabilir miydi? Ya da Cumhurbaşkanı Millî İstihbarat Teşkilatı Başkanına deseydi ki: Ya, bir gel de şu konuyu konuşalım. Sayın Cumhurbaşkanı, ben gelemiyorum, sana yardımcımı yollayacağım diyebilir miydi, bunu yapabilir miydi? Cumhurbaşkanına bunu yapamayan, millete bunu reva görüyor. Cumhurbaşkanına bu cüreti gösteremeyenler, milletin bütçesinin görüşüldüğü bu bütçe görüşmelerinde rahatlıkla buraya gelmemekle bu tavrı gösteriyorlar; ondan sonra da biz "tek adam rejimi" deyince kızıyorsunuz. Cumhurbaşkanı saraya çağırdığında -buradaki herkes o cevabı verecektir- Diyanet İşleri Başkanına "Gel." dediğinde koşa koşa gider ama bütçesinin hesabını vermeye buraya gelmiyor. Kıymetli bürokratlar var, dinlerler, giderler, aktarırlar ama bu hem bizlere saygısızlıktır hem milleti yok saymaktır, bu, iradeyi tanımamak anlamına gelir. Önümüzdeki sene de aynı tavrın aynı inatla gösterileceğine ben eminim çünkü artık bu bir kafa tutmadır, bunun başka bir anlamı, izahı açıklaması bize göre yoktur.
Bu ülkeye ve bu millete karşı sorumlusunuz, bu konuları bu şekilde ele alma mecburiyetiniz var; bu üç kurumun Başkanına söylüyorum bunları. Aslında Cumhurbaşkanlığı bütçesi, birazdan detaylarına ineceğimiz bu bütçe bir zihniyeti, Türkiye'deki mevcut Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sisteminin de karakterini tam olarak yansıtması açısından manidardır. Zira, baktığınız zaman, genel bütçenin binde 1'i bile etmeyen bir rakamdan bahsediyoruz ama temsil ettiği kurum ve içerisindeki detaylar açısından da çok anlam ifade ediyor. Zira, bize göre, baktığımız zaman, şeffaf olmayan, denetlenemeyen, hesap vermeyen, kamu ve STK'lerden çok kendi konfor ve itibarını düşünen, halktan kopuk, tutarsız bir yaklaşımın bu Cumhurbaşkanlığı bütçesinde tezahür ettiğini, samimiyetsizliğin, kendi koyduğu ilke ve kurallara, hedeflere uymayan, aynı Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sistemi gibi bir kaos ve yetki karmaşasının olduğunu görüyoruz biz bu bütçede. Bunları neden söylüyorum birazdan detaylarıyla sizlerle paylaşacağım. Ancak bundan önce, burada bir hakkı da teslim etmek durumundayım; zira, eleştiriyorsak zaman zaman memnun kaldığımız konularda da memnuniyetimizi ifade etmemiz eleştirilerimizin gücünün ve samimiyetinin ve tutarlılığının göstergesi açısından kıymetlidir, önemlidir. Sayın Cumhurbaşkanı Yardımcısı kendi konuşmasında da değindi, 1.426 soru önergesinin yaklaşık yüzde 90'ını cevaplamışlar ki bunun büyük bir kısmı on beş günlük süreden sonra cevaplanmış ama yüzde 90'ı cevaplanmış, geçen sene bu rakam da yüzde 88'di. Bakın, takdir etmiyorum, memnuniyetimi ifade ediyorum; zira, bu bir anayasal zorunluluk. Memnuniyetimizin sebebi de şudur: Diğer bakanlıklarla kıyasladığınızda, burada başarılı ya da dikkate alınan, kale aldığını gösteren bir anlayışı biz görüyoruz. Hele de Adalet Bakanlığının, İçişleri Bakanlığının vermiş olduğumuz soru önergelerini cevaplama oranına baktığınız zaman, Cumhurbaşkanı Yardımcısının ve ekibinin göstermiş olduğu titizlik memnuniyetimizi ifade etme mecburiyetini de beraber getiriyor. Tekrar söylüyorum, bu bir takdir değil; zira, anayasal olarak bu her bakanlığın sorumluluğu ama öyle bir hâle geldik ki yüzde 5'i, yüzde 10'u cevaplamayan bakanlıkların olduğu yerde böyle bir performans memnuniyeti de beraberinde ifade etmeyi getiriyor.
Şimdi, Cumhurbaşkanlığı bütçesi 21,2 milyar yani 18,9 trilyonluk genel toplam bütçenin içinde yüzde 0,11 yani binde 1, az önce de söyledim, büyüklüğünden çok, içeriği ve taşıdığı anlam ve topluma verdiği mesaj açısından bu bütçenin kıymeti var. Aslında diğer bağlı kurumlara baktığınız zaman da toplam rakam yaklaşık 627 milyar TL'lere geliyor yani Cumhurbaşkanlığına bağlı diğer kurumları da eklediğinizde o zaman da toplam bütçenin yüzde 3,3'ü gibi bir büyüklüğe tekabül ettiğini görüyoruz. Şimdi, yüzde 25'lik bir artış olduğundan bahsediliyor. Tabii, Cumhurbaşkanlığı bütçesinin bu yıl nasıl, hangi rakamla kapanacağını biz bilmiyoruz; zira, onuncu ay, Ekim ayı sonu itibarıyla baktığımızda, 2025 yılı için vermiş olduğunuz bütçenin yüzde 72'sini kullanmışsınız. Tabii, orada aysal, mevsimsel etkiler de olabilir ama şöyle bir tuhaflık da var: İşte, 600 milyon, 700 milyon harcanıyor,
harcanıyor, harcanıyor, Ekim ayında bir anda korkunç bir sıçrama var. Baktığınız zaman, şöyle bir hissiyata kapılıyorsunuz: Aman efendim, yıl sonu geliyor, biz bir an önce koyduğumuz bütçe rakamını kullanalım ki yıl sonunda herhangi bir şekilde kullanılmamış rakam dışarıda kalmasın. Böyle yapıyorsunuz demiyorum ama baktığımız zaman bize verdiği hissiyat budur. Bunu halletmenin, bunu bertaraf etmenin bir yolu var: Ya, burası Plan ve Bütçe Komisyonu, buraya Karayolları Trafik Kanunu geliyor, bilmiyorum sizin işiniz mi, Trafik Karayolları Kanununu buranın görüşmesi ama burası şunu görüşebilir: Üç ayda bir bu bütçelerin gerçekleşme ve bütçelenen rakam oranlarını Plan ve Bütçeye gönderebilirsiniz. Aylık verdiğiniz bütçelerin neden gerçekleştiğini, neden gerçekleşmediğini, nerede sapma olduğunu rahatlıkla ifade edebilirsiniz, Plan ve Bütçe Komisyonu da asli fonksiyonunu bana göre tam olarak o anlamda yerine getirmiş olur.
Personelde öngörülen yüzde 61'lik bir artış var. Bu artış nedir? Yani ilave personel mi alacaksınız, yoksa personele yapacağınız zam mıdır? Eğer öyleyse keşke bütün kamu görevlileri, memurlar da böyle bir oranla, yüzde 61'le karşılansa ama görünen o ki yüzde 61 demek ki ilave bir personel alımından bahsediliyor. Bunun detaylarını merak ediyoruz, belki vardı, biz gözden kaçırdık ama cevaplanırsa da memnun olacağız.
Mal ve hizmet alımı 11,7 milyar olarak bütçelenmiş. Tabii, bunu kalem kalem görmek istiyor insanlar, bizler. Şöyle bir yaklaşım içerisine de girmeyeceğim: Toplam bütçeyi alıp 365'e bölüp ondan sonra dönüp size, yahu, Cumhurbaşkanlığının günlük harcama kalemi buymuş demiyorum ama bunu dedirttiriyorsunuz. Getirin 11,7 milyarı yani ejder meyvesi mi alıyorsunuz, ne yapıyorsunuz görsün millet bunu. Siz bu açıklığı, bu şeffaflığı göstermezseniz, e, o zaman da muhalefet bu netliği görmediği zaman bu tür çıkarımlar içerisine girer ve oradan da "İşte, sarayın günlük harcaması şu kadardır." gibi bir mantığa yönelebilir. Dolayısıyla bunları kalem kalem göstermek... Hiçbir kötü niyetiniz olmayabilir ama bu şeffaflığın, bu denetlenebilir bir yapının mevcudiyetinin de ülke kadar sizlere de sizlerin kendinizi ifade etmesi açısından da faydası olduğunu görüyoruz.
Bütçede azalan tek rakam var, o da cari transferler. Şimdi, cari transferlerin detayına baktığımda da cari transferler genelde STK'ler için yani kamu yararına daha çok harcanan bir para gibi algılıyorum. Her rakam, her kalem artmış, STK'lere yapılacak yardımda geçen seneye göre yüzde 20 bir azalma olmuş. Yani buraya baktığınız zaman, kendi konforumuzdan, itibarımızdan bir tasarruf yok ama kamuya, STK'lere yapılacak "cari transfer" diye tanımlanan kalemden yüzde 20 bir azalma var gibi görüyoruz biz; bunlar açıklamaya muhtaç kalemler. Tabii, itibarla tasarruf olmuyor arkadaşlar. Bakın, yanımda bir görsel getirdim, çok manidar bulduğum için sizlerle de paylaşmak istiyorum yani itibarı buralarda aramanın gereği yok. Bakın, şu Japon Başbakanının Donald Trump'ı karşıladığı oda, üstündeki de Japon İmparatorunun Donald Trump'ı karşıladığı oda, sadece iki koltuk ve bir masa var. Yukarıdaki de Cumhurbaşkanının -öyle yabancı misyon falan değil ha- kendi ülkemizdeki yetkililerle yaptığı toplantı, bu, sadece külliyeden bir kare. Bakın, Japonya'nın millî geliri 4 trilyon dolar, bizim millî gelirimiz 1,3 trilyon dolar. Japonya'da kişi başına düşen millî gelir 32.500 dolar, bizde 13.400 dolar. Japonya'nın ihracatı yaklaşık 700 milyar dolar, bizim ihracatımız 262 milyar dolar yani itibar saraylarda, görkemde, şatafatta değil, itibar arıyorsak ülkemize, milletimize, devletimize, vatandaşımıza vereceğimiz en kıymetli itibar diğer ülkelerle kıyasladığımızda hem millî gelirin hem kişi başı millî gelirin hem ihracatımızın artacağı olması konusudur.
Şimdi, program sınıflandırmasına göre bütçe kalemlerine baktığımda benim gerçekten kafam karışıyor, hani diyorum ya bir kaos var diye. Sanayinin geliştirilmesi, üretim ve yatırımın desteklenmesi kalemi var, 965 milyonluk bir rakam konulmuş 2026 bütçesi için. E, bir de bizim bir Sanayi ve Teknoloji Bakanlığımız var değil mi? Yani
geçen sene Sanayi Bakanını dinledik Mecliste. Dinlediğimizde de ben dedim ki: Hükûmet Sanayi Bakanını evde unutmuş çünkü konuşmasının yüzde 90'ı teknolojiyle alakalıydı. Yani ülkenin sanayisi bitmiş, ülkenin sanayisi yanıyor; ne tür tedbir alınacak, ne yapılacak, hiçbir şey duymadık.
Şimdi, Sanayi Bakanlığının bütçesine baktığımız zaman da orada 89 milyarlık bir rakam var; bu da sanayinin geliştirilmesi, üretim ve yatırımın desteklenmesi için. Yani Sanayi ve Ticaret Bakanlığı bünyesinde 89 milyarlık bir kalem ayrılmışken Cumhurbaşkanlığının gene aynı başlıkta, gene aynı program dâhilinde bir rakam ayırmasının, 900 milyonluk bir rakam ayırmasının anlamı nedir? Burada bir verimlilik var mıdır, bir yetki karmaşası yok mudur? Hem Bakanlık hem Cumhurbaşkanlığı aynı başlıkta niye bütçe ayırır? Hani çaktırmadan hem annesinden hem babasından harçlık alan evlat gibi bir durum burada hissediyorsunuz, bunun detayları önemli.
Aynı durum Şehircilik ve Risk Odaklı Bütünleşik Afet Yönetim Programı'nda da var. Çevre ve Şehircilik Bakanlığında aynı başlıkta bir kalem var ki 2026 için 30 milyar bütçelenmiş, Cumhurbaşkanlığı da demiş ki "Ya, ben de koyuyorum, aynı başlıkla 800 milyonluk bir ödenek buraya ayırıyoruz." diye.
CUMHURBAŞKANI YARDIMCISI CEVDET YILMAZ - Aynı şeyler değil Değerli Vekilim, sadece farklı gösterimi.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (İstanbul) - Aynı şeyler olmayabilir ama başlıklar aynı. Yani burada işte Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sisteminin yani bir bakanlıkların, bir de işte başkanlıkların, ofislerin vesairelerin olduğu yerde aynı konuda, aynı bütçe rakamları bizim gibi herkesin kafasında soru işareti oluşturuyor. Elbette ki ayrı konulardır, başlıklar aynı; açıklamasını yaparsanız da memnun oluruz.
Bir de dış politika kalemi var. Tabii, neye göre, nasıl yapılıyor? Bakın, 2024 yılında 4,3 milyar bir bütçe öngörülmüş. Ne kadar harcamışsınız biliyor musunuz bu kaleme? 18 milyon harcanmış; sonra yıl sonunda bu 4 milyar Cumhurbaşkanlığına eklenmiş, Cumhurbaşkanlığı 4 milyar daha fazla harcamış, Dışişleri için koyduğunuz 4 milyar kullanılmamış, toplamda bütçenin içinde kalmışsınız ama bütçe içindeki kalemlerde ciddi savrulma var. Baktığınız zaman, ya bu kadar savrulma olur mu diyorsunuz yani 4,3 milyar deyip 18 milyon harcama nasıl yapılabilir? Aynı manzarayı bu sene de görüyoruz. Yaklaşık 6,2 milyar TL'lik bir bütçe konulmuş, ekim ayı sonu itibarıyla henüz 900 milyonluk bir harcama olmuş. Tahminen tabii, bunlar bir öngörüyle yapılıyor ama öngörülerin de tutmadığının, ne kadar saptığının bir göstergesi.
Bütçeye hazırlanırken ben bunlara baktığımda şöyle hissediyorum, belli ki birileri şöyle yapmış diyorum: "Yani biz bunları şöyle koyalım, buraya atalım da yıl sonu geldiğinde oranın ihtiyacı olur, buranın ihtiyacı olur, oradan alır bu tarafa kaydırırız." Kurumlar arasındaki bütçe kaymalarıyla ilgili -daha önce de bu Komisyonda konuşulmuş bu- bu kuralın çok açık, net konuşulması lazım. Zira bu aktarımlar farklı soru işaretlerini de beraberinde getiriyor.
Yani sistemin, Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sisteminin o kadar falsosu var ki yani bütçe kalemlerinde de görüyorsunuz. Bu falsonun, kaosun yarattığı karışıklıklar da var. Bakın, bir Adalet Bakanı var, bir de Adalet Bakanı gibi hareket eden bir Cumhurbaşkanı Başdanışmanı var. Türkiye'deki hukuki gündemi o Cumhurbaşkanı Başdanışmanı belirliyor adaletle ilgili konularda, biz oradan öğreniyoruz. İnşallah, Adalet Bakanı da kendisinden öğrenmiyordur hukukla ilgili ne olup biteceğini. Her konuda, her meselede bir içtihat yaratıyormuş gibi, Türkiye'de "hukuk" denilince herkesin açıp yorumlarını okuduğu ve tahminlerini beklediği bir isim hâline gelmiş bu danışman. Peki, o zaman Adalet Bakanını ne yapacağız? Yani sistemdeki karışıklığı, sistemdeki kaosu anlatmak için bu örnekleri sizlerle paylaşıyorum.
Başka iddialar var. İşte, Sayın Veli Ağbaba İstanbul Büyükşehir Belediyesiyle ilgili konuya değindi. Bütün bunların olduğu yerlerde, olduğu bir ortamda İstanbul Başsavcısının doğrudan Cumhurbaşkanıyla temasta olduğuna ve Adalet Bakanının da tamamen bu sürecin dışında olduğuna dair iddialar da peşinden tabii ki, elbette ki geliyor çünkü netlik yok. Herkes, danışman bir şey söylüyor, Bakan bir şey söylüyor; biri ötekinin yerini alır hâle geliyor yani biz bir kurumsal sorun yumağı görüyoruz.
Şimdi, gene bu sistemin getirdiği bana göre bir başka abukluk: Irak'taki bölgesel yönetimde şu anda aktif olarak bir statüsü olmayan -hiçbir statüsü yok resmî olarak- Mesud Barzani
Türkiye'ye geldi ve devlet protokolüyle karşılandı. Bakıyorsunuz, korumaları askerî üniforma giymiş, ellerinde uzun namlulu silahlar, akılları sıra burada bir güç gösterisi yapıyorlar; daha dün kırmızı pasaport alabilmek için bu devletin kapısını çalıyordu bunlar, bu isimler. Peki, ne oldu sonra? Bir başka Cumhurbaşkanı danışmanı daha var, bu sefer o çıktı sahneye ve dedi ki... Tabii, kim danışman, kim başdanışman onu da sayamıyoruz biz bu yapı, bu sistem içinde. Sayın Mustafa Akış, bu Mesut Barzani'nin ziyaretiyle ilgili şunları söylüyor bakın: "Davet edilmiş Mesut Barzani ve misafir edilmiş, misafirperver bir millet olarak bize düşen hoş geldiniz demektir fakat Türkiye Cumhuriyeti devletinin misafirlerine uyguladığı birtakım protokoller var..."
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN MEHMET MUŞ - Buyurun.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (İstanbul) - "... Mesut Barzani'nin şu an, Kuzey Irak yönetiminde de Irak Merkezî Hükûmetinde de herhangi bir görevi yok. Yani Abdullah Gül'e, Ahmet Necdet Sezer'e veya Binali Yıldırım'a yurt dışında uygulanan protokol neyse Sayın Barzani'ye de uygulanması gereken protokol aşağı yukarı odur." diyor, Cumhurbaşkanı danışmanı söylüyor bunu. "Ayrıca, bırakın eski bir devlet görevlisini, ülkemize bir devlet başkanı dahi gelse tören subayı, yaveri dışında korumaları asla askerî üniforma giyemez ve tabanca dışında uzun namlulu silahla görevini ifa edemez. Kaldı ki Türkiye Cumhuriyeti misafirini Şırnak'ta koruyacak kudrete de güce de kuvvete de sahiptir." İşte, çok övünülen Cumhurbaşkanlığı hükûmet sisteminin daha dün ortaya çıkardığı, hep beraber seyrettiğimiz resim. Ve sonunda da Şırnak Valiliğini etiketleyerek "Bu yaptığınız görüntüler uygun düşmemiş." diye yazı yazıyor. Sistemin sorunu da işte bize göre bu zaten. Valiler parti il başkanı gibi hareket edip kendine paye çıkarırsa, başdanışmanlar görüş bildirip siyasi bir çıkış noktası arayışına girerse "Tek parti görüntüsünün altında da o zaman ortada acaba bir saray entrikası mı var?" dersiniz, demek durumunda kalırsınız; bugün olan da budur.
Geçen sene bir sunum yaptınız, sunumunuzu ben okudum. O sunumda siber güvenlikle ilgili çok önemli bir başlık açmıştınız ki haklısınız, zira yaşadığımız rezaletler siber güvenlik konusunda ciddi tedbir almamız gerektiğini bize söylüyor. İşte, e-devletteki verilerin dışarıya sızdığı, birilerinin eline geçtiği iddiaları... Daha biz bunları konuşurken bir de üstüne sahte diplomalar, gene...
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (İstanbul) - Bitiriyorum Başkanım.
BAŞKAN MEHMET MUŞ - Sayın Kavuncu, bitti.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (İstanbul) - Bitiriyorum.
Son, selamlayıp bitiriyorum.
BAŞKAN MEHMET MUŞ - Buyurun, selamlayın.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (İstanbul) - Peki.
Önemli bir başlıktır ve Siber Güvenlik Başkanlığını da 2025 Ekim ayında yani geçtiğimiz ay itibarıyla açtınız yani kurulduğundan beri yaklaşık dokuz on ay geçmiş. İnşallah, şöyle bir şey olmamıştır: "Ya, biz bunu geçen sene söyledik, şimdi bize sorarlar; biz bunu apar topar açalım, ekim ayında kurdelesini keselim." Bu konuda da bu verilerle ilgili nasıl bir güvenlik tedbiri alacaksınız, neler yapılacak? Zira, anayasal olarak bu, devletin sorumluluğundadır; bizlerin verilerini korumak, onları kurtarmak, başkalarının eline geçmemesi de sizlerin yükümlülüğündedir.
BAŞKAN MEHMET MUŞ - Sayın Kısacık...
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (İstanbul) - Bitiriyorum.
Çok teşekkür ediyorum. Bütçenin hayırlı olmasını diliyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - Thank you.
Mr. President, Mr. Vice President, very valuable bureaucrats, dear members of parliament; First of all, the last day of the budget studies discussed in the Planning and Budget Commission was a very intense and grueling marathon. On this occasion, we would like to thank the Commission President, Commission members, stenographers, the staff who brought us tea, and everyone who contributed. Since today is the last day, we consider it a debt of gratitude. An important budget is being discussed; We are discussing the budget of the head of the executive branch. It seems that in the previous and next budgets, we will not see the head of the executive defending this budget. Because, since 2018, that is, since the day we moved to the Presidential government system, we have not witnessed Mr. President, the head of the executive, who is actually the main owner of the budget, coming to the Grand National Assembly of Turkey and participating in these negotiations in order to own this budget; This is a great deficiency. In our opinion, this is a political deficiency, a legal deficiency, a great administrative deficiency, and a very, very great deficiency in terms of the nation's budget right. In our opinion, the person who wants the authority to use the state's money should deign to come here and explain himself. Because the budget is honor, the budget is a very serious issue, both the Chairman of the party and the President; We are discussing an environment where state affairs and political affairs are intertwined through these budget discussions. All political activities are carried out while using the budget, so come and explain to this nation what kind of policy you will follow with this money and this budget, pen by pen. The minimum democratic requirement that makes visible the responsibility of the highest authority of the executive in the face of Parliamentary control is eliminated by the President's absence in person. Again, budget is not a technical issue; It is a political responsibility and program calculation. This issue is very, very important as it shows how meticulously you care about Bethlehem, how and at what level you protect the sustenance of the nation, it is a democratic minimum obligation.
You are looking for legitimacy; If you are looking for legitimacy, the place where you will find that legitimacy is these budget negotiations, where you discuss the nation's budget; The place where you will look for this legitimacy is not the words of Trump or the United States Secretary of State, but these discussions in the Turkish Grand National Assembly and your actual presence here will give you this legitimacy in the eyes of the nation. Budgets are the basic documents of politics; Budgets are perhaps the most important documents of politics, showing how a political structure views the world, events and national issues.
This issue was discussed again last year, and our Chairman of the Planning and Budget Commission, Mr. Erhan Usta, just brought it to the agenda; The President of Religious Affairs, the Director of National Intelligence and the Director of Communications were not there last year, not this year, and not the year before that. In our opinion, their failure to come to the Parliament, where they are supposed to be accountable, is an indication of how much they lack democratic consciousness and mentality, and a clear expression of how much they care about the will of the nation. Why do we care about this issue?

Look, let me ask you this, I'm asking all of you: For example, if the President had said
To the President of Religious Affairs: Come on, let's discuss a few topics regarding this budget. Could he send the Deputy President of Religious Affairs? Or if the President had said to the Head of the National Intelligence Organization: Come on, let's talk about this issue. Could Mr. President say, "I cannot come, I will send my assistant to you?" or could he do this? Those who cannot do this to the President see it as appropriate for the nation. Those who cannot show this courage to the President show this attitude by easily not coming here for these budget talks where the nation's budget is discussed; Then you get angry when we say "one man regime". When the President calls him to the palace - everyone here will give that answer - he says to the President of Religious Affairs, "Come." When he says that, he runs away, but he doesn't come here to account for his budget. There are valuable bureaucrats, they listen, go and report, but this is both disrespectful to us and ignoring the nation, which means not recognizing the will. I am sure that the same attitude will be shown with the same stubbornness next year, because this is now a defiance, we do not see any other meaning or explanation for this.
You are responsible to this country and this nation, you have an obligation to handle these issues in this way; I am telling these to the Presidents of these three institutions. In fact, the Presidential budget, which we will go into detail in a moment, is significant in that it fully reflects a mentality and the character of the current Presidential government system in Türkiye. Because, when you look at it, we are talking about a figure that is not even 1 per thousand of the general budget, but it means a lot in terms of the institution it represents and the details within it. Because, in our opinion, when we look at it, we see that an inconsistent, inconsistent approach that is not transparent, unmonitored, unaccountable, that cares more about its own comfort and reputation than the public and NGOs, that is disconnected from the public, is manifested in this Presidential budget, that there is insincerity, that does not comply with the principles and rules and targets set by itself, and that there is a chaos and confusion of authority just like the Presidential government system. I will share with you why I am saying these in detail in a moment. But before that, I have to make a point here; Because, if we criticize, it is valuable and important for us to express our satisfaction from time to time on issues that we are satisfied with, as it shows the strength, sincerity and consistency of our criticisms. Mr. Vice President also mentioned in his speech that they answered approximately 90 percent of the 1,426 parliamentary questions, most of which were answered after the fifteen-day period, but 90 percent were answered, last year this figure was 88 percent. Look, I don't appreciate it, I express my satisfaction; because this is a constitutional obligation. The reason for our satisfaction is this: When you compare it with other ministries, we see an understanding here that is successful or taken into consideration and shows that it is taken into account.

Especially when you look at the response rate of the Ministry of Justice and the Ministry of Internal Affairs to the parliamentary questions we submitted, the diligence shown by the Vice President and his team brings with it the obligation to express our satisfaction. Again, this is not an appreciation; Because, constitutionally, this is the responsibility of every ministry, but we have come to such a point that where there are ministries that do not answer 5 percent or 10 percent, such performance satisfaction brings with it expression.
Now, the Presidential budget is 21.2 billion, that is, 0.11 percent of the general total budget of 18.9 trillion, that is, 1 per thousand. As I have just said, this budget has value in terms of its content, the meaning it carries and the message it gives to the society, rather than its size. In fact, when you look at other affiliated institutions, the total figure comes to approximately 627 billion TL. That is, when you add other institutions affiliated with the Presidency, we see that it corresponds to 3.3 percent of the total budget. Now, there is talk of a 25 percent increase. Of course, we do not know how and with what figure the Presidential budget will be closed this year; Because, when we look at the tenth month, at the end of October, you have used 72 percent of the budget you gave for 2025. Of course, there may be lunar and seasonal effects, but there is also this strangeness: Here, 600 million, 700 million is spent,
It is spent, it is spent, suddenly there is a terrible jump in October. When you look at it, you get the feeling: Oh, sir, the end of the year is coming, let's use the budget figure we set as soon as possible, so that no unused numbers are left out at the end of the year. I'm not saying you do it like this, but this is the feeling it gives us when we look at it. There is a way to handle this, to eliminate this: Well, this is the Plan and Budget Commission, the Highway Traffic Law comes here, I don't know if it is your job, the Traffic and Highways Law is discussed here, but this place can discuss the following: You can send the realization and budgeted figures of these budgets to the Plan and Budget every three months. You can easily express why your monthly budgets were realized, why they were not realized, where there was a deviation, and in my opinion, the Plan and Budget Commission has fulfilled its primary function in that sense.
There is a projected 61 percent increase in staff. What is this increase? So, will you hire additional staff or will you raise the staff? If so, I wish all public officials and civil servants were met with such a rate, 61 percent, but it seems that 61 percent means that they are talking about hiring additional personnel. We are curious about the details of this, maybe there was one, we missed it, but we will be pleased if it is answered.
The purchase of goods and services was budgeted at 11.7 billion. Of course, people want to see this in pencil, us. I will not take such an approach: You take the total budget, divide it by 365, and then turn around and say, well, I am not saying that this is the daily expenditure item of the Presidency, but you are making you say this. Bring 11.7 billion, that is, you are buying dragon fruit, let people see what you are doing.

If you do not show this openness, this transparency, then when the opposition does not see this clarity, they will make such inferences and from there they will say, "Here, the daily expenditure of the palace is this much." It may lead to a logic like this. Therefore, showing them pencil by pencil. You may not have any bad intentions, but we see that this transparency and the existence of an auditable structure is beneficial to you, as well as to the country, in terms of expressing yourself.
There is only one decreasing figure in the budget, and that is current transfers. Now, when I look at the details of current transfers, I perceive that current transfers are generally money spent for NGOs, that is, for the public good. Every number, every item has increased, and there has been a 20 percent decrease in aid to NGOs compared to last year. So when you look here, there is no saving on our own comfort or reputation, but we see that there is a 20 percent decrease in the item defined as "current transfers" to the public and NGOs; These are items that need explanation. Of course, friends, reputation does not save money. Look, I brought an image with me, I want to share it with you because I find it very meaningful, so there is no need to look for reputation here. Look, that room where the Japanese Prime Minister welcomed Donald Trump, and above that the room where the Japanese Emperor welcomed Donald Trump, there are only two chairs and a table. Above is the meeting of the President - not a foreign mission or anything - with the officials in our own country, this is just a shot from the complex. Look, Japan's national income is 4 trillion dollars, our national income is 1.3 trillion dollars. The national income per capita in Japan is 32,500 dollars, in our country it is 13,400 dollars. Japan's exports are approximately 700 billion dollars, our exports are 262 billion dollars, that is, if we are looking for reputation, not in palaces, splendor and pomp, the most valuable credit we can give to our country, our nation, our state and our citizens is that both national income, national income per capita and our exports will increase when compared to other countries.
Now, when I look at the budget items according to program classification, I get really confused, as I say, there is chaos. There is an item to develop industry, support production and investment, a figure of 965 million has been set for the 2026 budget. Well, we also have a Ministry of Industry and Technology, right? So
Last year, we listened to the Minister of Industry in the Parliament. When we listened, I said: The government forgot the Minister of Industry at home because 90 percent of his speech was about technology. In other words, the country's industry is finished, the country's industry is burning; We haven't heard anything about what kind of precautions will be taken or what will be done.
Now, when we look at the budget of the Ministry of Industry, there is a figure of 89 billion; This is to develop industry and support production and investment. In other words, while an item of 89 billion has been allocated within the Ministry of Industry and Trade, what does it mean that the Presidency allocates a figure of 900 million under the same title, within the same program? Is there any efficiency here, is there not a confusion of authority? Why do both the Ministry and the Presidency allocate budgets under the same title?

You feel like a child who secretly receives pocket money from both his mother and father. The details of this are important.
The same situation exists in the Urbanization and Risk-Oriented Integrated Disaster Management Program. There is an item under the same title in the Ministry of Environment and Urbanization, which has a budget of 30 billion for 2026, and the Presidency said, "Well, I put it there too, we are allocating an allowance of 800 million under the same title." saying.
VICE PRESIDENT CEVDET YILMAZ - They are not the same, Dear Deputy, just different representations.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - They may not be the same, but the titles are the same. So, here, in the Presidential government system, where there are ministries, presidencies, offices, etc., the same budget numbers on the same issue create a question mark in the minds of everyone like us. Of course, they are separate topics, the titles are the same; We would be pleased if you could explain.
There is also a foreign policy item. Of course, on what basis and how is it done? Look, a budget of 4.3 billion is envisaged in 2024. Do you know how much you spent on this pen? 18 million spent; Then, at the end of the year, this 4 billion was added to the Presidency, the Presidency spent 4 billion more, the 4 billion you put for Foreign Affairs was not used, you remained within the budget in total, but there is a serious deviation in the items in the budget. When you look at it, you wonder if there will be such a swing, how can you spend 18 million instead of 4.3 billion? We see the same view this year. A budget of approximately 6.2 billion TL was set, and as of the end of October, there was an expenditure of 900 million. Of course, these are made with a prediction, but it is an indication of how the predictions did not come true and how much they deviated.
When I look at these while preparing the budget, I feel like this, obviously someone did it like this: "So let's put them like this, let's throw them here, so that when the end of the year comes, there will be a need there, there will be a need here, we will take them from there and move them this way." This rule regarding budget shifts between institutions - which has been discussed before in this Commission - needs to be discussed very clearly. Because these transfers bring with them different question marks.
In other words, the system, the Presidential government system, has so many flaws that you see it in the budget items. There are also complications created by this distortion and chaos. Look, there is a Minister of Justice, and there is a Chief Advisor to the President who acts like the Minister of Justice. The Chief Advisor to the President determines the legal agenda in Türkiye on matters related to justice, we learn from there. Hopefully, the Minister of Justice does not learn from him what will happen regarding the law. This consultant has become a name that everyone reads his comments and awaits his predictions when "law" is mentioned in Türkiye, as if he creates jurisprudence on every subject and every issue. So, what do we do with the Minister of Justice then? In other words, I am sharing these examples with you to explain the confusion and chaos in the system.
There are other claims. Here, Mr. Veli Ağbaba touched upon the issue regarding Istanbul Metropolitan Municipality.

In places where all these are happening, of course, there are also allegations that the Istanbul Chief Public Prosecutor is in direct contact with the President and that the Minister of Justice is completely outside of this process, because there is no clarity. Everyone, the consultant says something, the Minister says something; One begins to replace the other, so we see a tangle of institutional problems.
Now, another absurdity in my opinion brought about by this system: Massoud Barzani, who currently does not have an active status in the regional administration in Iraq - he has no status officially.
He came to Türkiye and was welcomed with state protocol. You see, his guards are dressed in military uniforms, with long-barreled weapons in their hands, and they are making a show of power here; Just yesterday, these people were knocking on the door of this state to get a red passport. So, what happened next? There is another advisor to the President, this time he came on stage and said. Of course, we cannot count who is an advisor and who is a chief advisor within this structure, this system. See what Mr. Mustafa Akis says about Mesut Barzani's visit: "As an invited, hospitable nation, Mesut Barzani is invited, and our duty is to welcome him, but there are some protocols that the Republic of Turkey imposes on its guests."
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Here you go.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - ". Currently, Mesut Barzani does not have any duty in the Northern Iraq administration or the Iraqi Central Government. In other words, the protocol that is applied abroad to Abdullah Gül, Ahmet Necdet Sezer or Binali Yıldırım is more or less the same protocol that should be applied to Mr. Barzani." says, the President's advisor says this. "Also, even if a head of state comes to our country, let alone a former government official, his bodyguards, other than the ceremonial officer and his aide, can never wear military uniforms and cannot perform their duties with a long-barreled weapon other than a pistol. Moreover, the Republic of Turkey has the power and power to protect its guests in Şırnak." Here is the picture we all watched together, which was revealed just yesterday by the much-vaunted Presidential government system. And finally, he tagged Şırnak Governorship and said, "These images you made are not appropriate." he writes. In our opinion, this is the problem with the system. If the governors act as party provincial heads and gain honor for themselves, if the chief advisors express their opinions and seek a political starting point, "Is there a palace intrigue underneath the single-party image?" You say, you have to say; That's what's happening today.
You made a presentation last year, I read your presentation. In that presentation, you opened a very important topic about cyber security, and you are right, because the disgraces we have experienced tell us that we need to take serious measures regarding cyber security. Here are the allegations that the data in e-government has leaked out and been taken into someone's hands. While we are talking about these, fake diplomas again.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - I am finishing, President.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Mr. Kavuncu, it is over.

MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - I am finishing.
Lastly, I salute and end.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Come on, greet me.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - Okay.
It is an important topic, and you opened the Cyber ​​Security Presidency in October 2025, that is, as of last month, approximately nine or ten months have passed since its establishment. Hopefully, something like this did not happen: "Well, we said this last year, now they will ask us; let's open it quickly and cut the ribbon in October." In this regard, what security measures will you take regarding this data, what will be done? Because, constitutionally, this is the responsibility of the state; It is your responsibility to protect our data, save it, and prevent it from falling into the hands of others.
MAYOR MEHMET MUŞ - Mr Kısacık.
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - I am finishing.
Thank you very much. I hope the budget will be beneficial.

records.title: Tutanak Metni
records.originalTurkish:
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (İstanbul) - Değerli Başkan, sayın milletvekilleri; hepinizi saygıyla selamlıyorum.

Çok önemli bir teklifi görüşüyoruz. Tabii, hepimizin üzerinde hemfikir olduğu net bir konu var, bir problemimiz var ve popülasyonun azaltılmasıyla alakalı da bir tedbir alınması konusunda herkes hemfikir. Ayrıldığımız nokta şu: Bir taraf, benim de içinde olduğum taraf diyor ki: Bu hayvanları katletmeden, öldürmeden bu probleme biz çözebiliriz. Bir diğer tarafta diyor ki: Öldürmeden bu problemi çözebilme imkânı yok. Şimdi karşımıza o fotoğrafları koymuşlar. Hepimiz üzülüyoruz, bütün mağdurlara geçmiş olsun, hayatını kaybedenlere Allah'tan rahmet diliyorum ama bakın, o fotoğrafları gördüğümüzde içimiz acıdığı kadar yaşadığımız bir başka duygu da nedir biliyor musunuz? Şudur: Bu iktidar, yirmi iki yıldır bu ülkeyi yönetenler bu mağduriyetlerin sebebi olmuştur. Kendi kusurlarınızı, kendi ayıplarınızı sergiliyorsunuz, hepimizin vicdanı sızlıyor, hiç kimse bu mağduriyetten dolayı mutlu değil ama problemin hayvanlar katledilmeden, sokak hayvanları öldürülmeden de çözülebilme imkânı olduğunu söylüyoruz. İçeride varsa STK, farklı düşünen dışarıdakiler de dinlensin. Evet, usul konusundaki itirazlara da katılıyoruz. Elbette ki insanımızı, çocuklarımızı, doğamızı yaşatmak arzusundayız ama bu hadiseyi çocukların sokak hayvanları tarafından mağdur edilmesine, katledilmesine, sessiz kalanlar, göz yumanlar ve göz yummayanlar diye ayırt ederseniz o zaman da biz çıkar geçmişte yaşanılan çocuk tecavüzlerinden vakıflarda yaşanan rezilliklere kadar bunların hepsini burada gündeme getiririz. Hiç kimse bunlardan memnun olmaz. Dolayısıyla, bu işi sabırla, hayvanları katletmeden eğer çözebilme imkânı varsa ve bu imkânı zorlamıyorsanız bunun vebali hepinizin üzerinde olacaktır.
records.englishTranslation:
MEHMET SATUK BUĞRA KAVUNCU (Istanbul) - Dear President, dear members of parliament; I greet you all with respect.

We are discussing a very important offer. Of course, there is one clear issue that we all agree on, we have a problem and everyone agrees that measures should be taken to reduce the population. The point where we differ is this: One side, including me, says: We can solve this problem without slaughtering or killing these animals. On the other hand, he says: There is no way to solve this problem without killing. Now they put those photos in front of us. We all feel sorry, I wish all the victims a speedy recovery, and I wish God's mercy to those who lost their lives, but look, do you know what is another feeling we experience when we see those photos, as much as it hurts? It is this: This government, those who have been ruling this country for twenty-two years, have been the cause of these grievances. You are displaying your own flaws, your own shame, all of our conscience hurts, no one is happy about this victimization, but we say that the problem can be solved without slaughtering animals or killing stray animals. If there are NGOs inside, those outside who think differently should also be listened to. Yes, we also agree with the objections regarding the procedure. Of course, we want to keep our people, our children and our nature alive, but if you separate this incident into those who remain silent, those who turn a blind eye, and those who do not turn a blind eye to the victimization and murder of children by stray animals, then we will bring all of these to the agenda here, from child rapes in the past to the disgrace experienced in foundations. No one will be happy with these. Therefore, if it is possible to solve this problem patiently and without slaughtering animals, and if you do not force this possibility, the blame for this will be on all of you.
